title: Sprint authoring through the helper — whole-file write and step_log append
description: Teach the helper to write sprint records (`kind=sprint`): whole-sprint create/update (scribe owns `sprint_<N>.json` at open/close) AND a `step_log` append mode (a gate adds one verdict without rewriting the file). Emit canonical-sentinel form — accreting arrays last, a constant sentinel key dead-last — so concurrent appends produce minimal, non-conflicting diffs. Expose `to-execution sprint ...`.
acceptance_criteria: `to-execution sprint` can create/update a whole sprint record and append a single `step_log` entry
Sprint files are emitted in canonical-sentinel form (arrays last, sentinel key dead-last) by a shared serializer
A single step_log append is a minimal byte-diff that does not touch sibling entries — sprint-4 step_log lesson
Whole-sprint authoring (open/close) goes through the helper, not just appends — H5
Sentinel-serializer constants are named, not inline — sprint-5 lesson
